headline: A Stolen Bike, a Cancer Patient, and One Cop's Month-Long Promise
meta_description: Officer JP Young spent a month searching for a cancer survivor's stolen e-bike. When he finally found it, his joy was unmistakable.
summary_short: Officer JP Young promised to find Olivia's stolen e-bike after it vanished on Mother's Day. One month later, he delivered—and the community rallied behind her.
body:
On Mother's Day, someone stole the three-wheeled electric bike that Olivia, a cancer survivor, relied on to reach her oncology appointments. Officer JP Young of the Kansas City Police Department made her a simple promise: he would do everything he could to find it.

For the next month, he kept his word. Young and his partner spotted a man riding an e-bike with fresh spray paint, but traces of the old paint still visible beneath. Young snapped a photo and texted it to Olivia. She confirmed it immediately—it was her bike.

When the officer returned the trike, Olivia noticed something that moved her deeply: the biggest smile on his face. "Officer Young went way beyond the call of duty," she wrote on the KCPD Facebook post. "Not only did he keep a steadfast eye out for it, he patiently tolerated all of my texts."

But the story didn't end there. Friends had bought Olivia a replacement bike, but it needed repairs she couldn't afford. A local Urban Trail Co. volunteer saw the reunion post and immediately offered to fix the recovered bike at no cost. "Bikes are part of our lives," they wrote, "and some of us would be happy to help her get rolling again."

One commenter summed it up simply: "Your generosity has brought tears to my eyes."
